pages.raw_text:
The two new pumps ordered last
spring for the up-river pumping station
have been installed, and test shows
that each works to nicely, and is cap
able of pumping its maximum quantity
of water per day, $500, 900 gallons.


Both pumps were attached to the
same journal and test made this
week shows that the full complement
of pumps at the power station can
work to maximum capacity with but
half Of the total water power Roadway
turned on. 1t has been demonstrated
that the pumps if necessary can throw
the total estimated capacity per day,
20,000.000 gallons, into the city mains.
This is the full capacity of the mains.


During the past two weeks the city
has been using ON an average 5000, 900
gallons per day. Accordingly the new
pumps will not be required to work
